creator: jilles@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Fri, Dec 09, 2011 at 07:04:36AM -0500, Michael Scheidell wrote:
> Could you confirm this?

> you are running on i386, and this patch affects everything BUT i386, right?

The patch affects only i386. All other architectures already have -fPIC.

> if this is compiled on i386, the patch is a no-op.  it did not change 
> anything. right?

On i386 it gets rid of the text relocations.

Note that the CPPFLAGS+=       -fPIC line remains.

> it will, however change amd64/sparc/int64, everything else?

> have you tried this on amd64?

I have not tried it but it should be unaffected.

> and, in fact, your notes seem to carry a warning:

> While it works to add non-PIC objects to a shared object on i386
> (different from amd64 where it does not work), this is not a good idea.

-- 
Jilles Tjoelker
